title: bidens aurea var. wrightii
intro: Bidens aurea var. wrightii, also known as Wright's tickseed or Golden Beggar-ticks, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Bidens aurea var. wrightii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
